definition: VP's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient is the sole enabling technique: it preserves inputs whose intercepted comparison operands move incrementally closer to the gate target, outperforming both naive (no gradient at all) and value_profile_cmplog (gradient plus I2S, where the added I2S stage regresses performance by diluting the corpus or consuming per-execution budget). Gate structures range from simple equality to switch-case dispatch to bitmask presence; the common thread is that the CMP_MAP bucket signal is both necessary and sufficient while I2S is actively counterproductive. These are MIXED-edge cases (vp>naive AND vp>vpc) requiring manual review to confirm whether the anti-I2S edge is a consistent VP_pro-with-I2S-overhead phenomenon rather than noise.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient preserves inputs scoring incremental Hamming-distance progress toward GPOS comparison values, ratcheting toward the structural flag state that naive cannot sense and that vpc's added I2S layer does not improve.
signature.one_line: VP CMP gradient climbs deep GPOS attachment flag state unreachable by naive

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be a font whose GPOS table sets HB_BUFFER_SCRATCH_FLAG_HAS_GPOS_ATTACHMENT, i.e. drives shaping deep enough to produce a glyph attachment.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A font input that passes enough CMP-distance milestones during GPOS table parsing/shaping to set the GPOS-attachment scratch flag; the true branch is reached only after the buffer accumulates a GPOS attachment, a deep semantic state behind many integer/tag comparisons.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves 9/10 and hits the branch 105 times on average; its CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback keeps near-match font inputs whose operands approach the gate values, ratcheting toward the kern/GPOS structure that triggers the attachment flag.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 (15 hits) because it has only edge feedback and no gradient toward the comparison milestones; value_profile_cmplog blocks 8/10 (6 hits) because adding the per-execution CMP buffer and I2S stage diverts energy without helping reach this structural flag, so it underperforms plain value_profile.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets) preserves inputs that get progressively closer to the comparison values guarding GPOS parsing, a feedback advantage naive lacks and which the extra I2S/CMP-buffer machinery in value_profile_cmplog does not improve.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true branch is a GPOS-attachment scratch-flag test, a deep semantic state rather than a single CMP magic value.
Taking the true branch leads into the attachment-offset propagation loop that the winners execute and losers do not.
value_profile beats naive on this branch with much higher hitcount.
value_profile resolves 9/10 while naive blocks 8/10 and hits the branch only 15 times on average.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=105  loser=15

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile also beats value_profile_cmplog, so adding I2S to value_profile does not help reach this branch.
value_profile_cmplog is the loser of pair 2, hitting the branch only 6 times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=105  loser=6

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's mechanism is a CMP_MAP distance-bucket gradient that preserves near-match inputs.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: minimizer beats naive via a calibration-derived energy advantage.
minimizer reaches the branch fastest with the highest hitcount and shortest blocked duration.
minimizer derives per-seed mutation energy from calibration, favoring faster/smaller/more-stable seeds.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
faster, smaller,
more-stable seeds earn more mutations

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx beats naive via context-sensitive coverage.
naive_ctx folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index so the same edge counts at different slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it

evidence_trail.claim: The branch sits at the bottom of a deep multi-hop shaping call chain, so new call-contexts along it are plentiful.
naive has only edge feedback and no I2S or comparison introspection.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: Winning seeds are well-formed font inputs containing the kern table tag, evidencing real font structure reaching shaping.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a single fixed-width integer CMP (no deep parse state) where value_profile shows no advantage over naive AND value_profile_cmplog matches or beats value_profile, contradicting the observed vp>vpc and vp>naive ordering.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact comparison operands that value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient ratchets toward are not directly observed; the BYTE DIFF shows differing tag bytes (0x0c-0x0f) but the link from those bytes to the attachment-flag-setting GPOS parse is inferred from the call chain rather than a single decisive CMP.

signature.mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient rewards incremental byte moves toward a syntactically valid bracketed-SQL token containing '['; naive lacks this signal; vpc also has CMP_MAP but I2S adds overhead that regresses it, so the gradient alone enables the win.
signature.one_line: VP CMP gradient builds valid SQL bracket token; I2S overhead hurts vpc

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain a SQL token quoted with '[' so the dequoted first char equals '[' at sqlite3Dequote.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A SQL string containing a bracket-quoted identifier whose opening quote char is the literal '[' (0x5b), so that after sqlite3Isquote accepts the quote, the byte tested at line 31402 equals '['. The winner must drive an executable input that reaches Dequote with z[0]=='['.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner-resolving seed is a valid SELECT statement carrying a bracketed token '[13-3]' — byte 0x5b('[') sits at offset 0x1c and the closing 0x5d(']') at offset 0x21 — so when this token is dequoted, quote=='[' and the true branch is taken (winner hitcount=2 on the branch).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds are short, malformed fragments (e.g. ';d T 1S', 'T 1; c BINARY') with no '[' opening a quoted token; at the blocker line the losers never satisfy quote=='[' (L side L31402 shows T=0 F=20) and never even parse a valid bracketed identifier, so the equality byte is never produced.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose CMP operands move closer to matching: as havoc mutates bytes toward the syntactically-valid bracketed-SELECT shape, the per-CMP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets register incremental progress and preserve the partial inputs, letting value_profile climb to a parseable '[...]' token. naive lacks CMP_MAP feedback so it gets no gradient signal; in pair 2 value_profile_cmplog additionally has I2S yet still loses, showing the enabling technique is the CMP_MAP gradient (shared by both winner-pairs), not I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: Taking the blocked true side requires the dequote quote char to be '['.
value_profile is the winner over both naive and value_profile_cmplog at this branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: winner (value_profile vs naive); winner (I2S vs value_profile_cmplog)

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving seed is a SELECT statement containing a bracketed token, and it was found by value_profile.
The winning seed carries the closing bracket ']' completing the bracketed token.
The byte diff shows the winner uniquely carries '[' at offset 0x1c and ']' at 0x21 where the losers do not.
At the blocker line the loser inputs never satisfy quote=='[' (true side never taken on L).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L31402  T=1 F=1  T=0 F=20  if( quote=='[' ) quote = ']';  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has CMP_MAP gradient feedback that preserves inputs whose CMP operands approach matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace, so its advantage is not input-to-state substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on quote=='[' where value_profile and naive resolve the branch at statistically indistinguishable rates (no CMP_MAP gradient advantage), which would show the bracket gate is not what value_profile's distance-bucket feedback is solving.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Only one value_profile winner-resolving seed is shown, so the inference that the '[' bracketed token is the necessary feature (rather than incidental to a larger valid-SQL requirement) rests on a single positive example.

members.source_line: case OP_Le:               /* same as TK_LE, jump, in1, in3 */

signature.mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback over intercepted VDBE integer/string CMPs incrementally builds a valid relational SQL expression; naive has only edge counters; vpc has CMP_MAP too but its added I2S corpus dilution regresses it, isolating CMP_MAP gradient as the decisive factor.
signature.one_line: VP CMP gradient assembles relational SQL opcode; I2S dilution stalls vpc

analysis.summary_one_line: A valid SQL query containing a '<=' (or sibling) comparison whose numeric operands actually compare, reaching the OP_Le VDBE case jump.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An ASCII SQL statement that parses into a VDBE program containing an OP_Le/OP_Lt/OP_Ge comparison opcode whose two non-NULL operands are evaluated by the integer/MemCompare path, so the comparison case executes its true (jump) side. The winning seed 'SELECT 11-013>=1...' contains explicit relational operators including a '<=' (bytes 3c 3d) and arithmetic that yields comparable integer operands.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves the branch 8/10. Its corpus contains the structurally valid SQL seed 'SELECT 11-013>=1 3=13-3-323=13=13-3-32=1<=13-3-32=...' which carries the '<=' relational operator (W byte 3c('<') at offset 0x27) plus integer operands; the CMP_MAP gradient feedback over the VDBE integer/string comparisons rewards mutants that drive comparison operands closer, building the valid relational expression incrementally.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive (loser in pair 1) never resolves (0/10): with only edge-counter feedback it must assemble a valid relational SQL expression by blind havoc/token mutation, its blocking seeds are short garbage like ';d T 1S' and 'T 1; c BINARY' that never parse into an OP_Le comparison opcode. value_profile_cmplog (loser in pair 2) also stalls (1/10) despite having the CMP_MAP gradient, because adding the I2S CmpLog stage and per-execution CMP buffering dilutes its corpus and per-exec budget here rather than helping reach this comparison opcode.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets over intercepted CMPs) provides progress feedback toward forming a comparable relational expression that naive's plain edge counters cannot sense; the I2S CmpLog technique that distinguishes vpc from vp does not help and the vp-only arm wins, so the enabling technique is the CMP_MAP gradient, not I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the OP_Le comparison-opcode case in the VDBE interpreter, true (jump) side globally blocked.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line: case OP_Le:               /* same as TK_LE, jump, in1, in3 */
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Reaching the comparison body requires neither operand to be NULL and runs a real integer or MemCompare comparison.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: /* Neither operand is NULL.  Do a comparison. */

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile is the decisive winner in both pairs, resolving 8/10 while naive resolves 0/10 and value_profile_cmplog resolves 1/10.
Pair 2 is an I2S delta where value_profile beats value_profile_cmplog, so removing I2S helps.
The winner-resolving seed is a valid SQL SELECT statement carrying relational comparison operators.
The winning seed contains a '<=' relational operator (bytes 3c 3d).
At offset 0x27 the winner byte is '<' while the losers carry unrelated bytes, marking the relational operator as a discriminating feature.
The loser-blocking seeds are short non-parseable fragments that do not form a relational comparison.
value_profile feedback rewards an input whose CMP-operand pair is closer to matching than any previously seen, the CMP_MAP gradient mechanism.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only per-edge hit-count feedback and no CMP introspection.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2S CmpLog stage and per-execution CMP buffering on top of the CMP_MAP gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Instrumentation**: union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness that gates the OP_Le-equivalent comparison only behind a valid relational SQL expression and finds naive resolving it as fast as value_profile (no CMP_MAP-gradient advantage), or value_profile_cmplog resolving it strictly faster than value_profile.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the CMP_MAP gradient (vs. mere corpus-quality / valid-SQL bias) is the actual driver is inferential — the prompt shows value_profile wins but does not directly trace a gradient walk on the comparison operands.

signature.mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient credits partial progress toward valid comparison-operator bytes across the SQL parse path, steering the corpus toward the TK_LE switch arm; naive lacks CMP_MAP; vpc's I2S stage regresses performance, confirming CMP_MAP gradient (not I2S) is responsible.
signature.one_line: VP CMP gradient steers parser to TK_LE arm; vpc I2S stage regresses

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be a SQL relational-comparison expression (e.g. '<=', '>=') so resolveExprStep reaches the TK_LE comparison-operator switch arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes must parse into a SQL expression containing a relational comparison operator (TK_LE/TK_LT/TK_GE/TK_GT family, e.g. ASCII '<=' or '>=' between numeric operands), so the parsed Expr->op lands on the TK_LE case of the switch in resolveExprStep rather than on TK_ID/TK_VARIABLE/junk arms.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winning value_profile seed is 'SELECT 11-013>=13...' packed with '>=', '<=', and '=' comparison operators over numeric operands, forming valid comparison expressions whose Expr->op reaches the TK_LE/TK_GE switch arm (T=1 hits on the blocker line under W).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are degenerate 'SELECT +:SELECT...' / 'SELECT +LLLLLT...' fragments with no well-formed relational comparison; their expressions resolve down the TK_ID / TK_VARIABLE arms (L hits L98354 TK_ID T=5, L98632 TK_VARIABLE T=9) and never the TK_LE arm (L T=0 at L98662).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose intercepted integer/string CMP operands move closer to matching the parser's keyword/operator-token comparisons, giving partial-progress credit that lets it assemble a valid comparison expression. Both decisive pairs have value_profile as winner: it beats naive (which lacks CMP_MAP) and beats value_profile_cmplog (where adding the I2S CmpLog stage regresses, so the win is attributable to the CMP_MAP gradient, not I2S).

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the TK_LE arm of a relational-comparison operator switch in resolveExprStep, globally blocked on the true side.
The TK_LE blocker sits among the relational comparison cases TK_EQ/TK_NE/TK_LT/TK_GT/TK_GE.
The winning value_profile seed is a SQL SELECT containing relational comparison operators over numeric operands.
The winning seed contains a '<=' relational comparison.
The loser-blocking seeds are degenerate SELECT fragments without well-formed relational comparison expressions.
Under the loser-blocking seed set, the parser resolves expressions down the TK_ID and TK_VARIABLE arms but never the TK_LE arm.
Loser-blocking inputs take the TK_VARIABLE arm 9 times while the blocker TK_LE true side is never taken under L.
value_profile wins over naive (delta value_profile) and over value_profile_cmplog (delta I2S), so value_profile is the winner in both decisive pairs.
value_profile's distinguishing technique is CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewarding operand pairs closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, so its win cannot be attributed to I2S substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only gate is reaching the TK_LE comparison arm shows naive resolving it as fast as value_profile (no CMP_MAP advantage), or value_profile_cmplog matching value_profile — which would refute that CMP_MAP gradient is the decisive feature.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Only one winning seed is available, so the claim that the CMP_MAP gradient (rather than incidental havoc luck on numeric-comparison bytes) is what assembles the comparison expression rests on the technique-delta inference more than on direct seed-lineage evidence.

signature.mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP writes a (PC, operand-distance bucket) entry per intercepted integer CMP, preserving seeds whose operand pair approaches the threshold; naive has no such feedback; vpc's substitution stage is counterproductive, so dispersed operator-byte diversity driven by CMP_MAP gradient alone crosses the rare true branch.
signature.one_line: VP CMP bucket feedback walks dispersed operator bytes to rare true branch

analysis.summary_one_line: A SELECT expression with diverse comparison/arithmetic operators that drives an integer-CMP operand pair toward equality, reachable via CMP_MAP gradient feedback.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A SELECT query whose expression body uses a varied mix of relational/arithmetic operators (=, >=, <=, -, /) at offsets 0x06-0x2d, so that an integer comparison inside sqlite3ExprCodeTarget produces a near-matching operand pair (the rare true branch at line 103533, T=1/F=62 in W).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner seed is 'SELECT 11-013>=1 3=13-3-323=13=13 ...' with distinct single-byte operators per offset (3e(>),3d(=),2d(-),2f(/),3c(<)), exactly the operand-distance gradient that CMP_MAP rewards; W resolves 9/10 and is the only side that takes the blocker true branch (W T=1 F=62 vs L T=0 F=743).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Both losers (naive, value_profile_cmplog) stay blocked: their inputs are degenerate digit/operator runs ('SELECT 222&22222...', '2222222222222222') concentrated on '2'(0x32), '&'(0x26) and '1'(0x31) across offsets 0x07-0x2d, never assembling the operator diversity that nudges the CMP operand pair across the threshold; naive has no CMP feedback at all and value_profile_cmplog's added substitution stage diverts mutation effort away from the gradient.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: each intercepted integer CMP writes a (PC, operand-distance bucket) entry, so an input producing operands closer to matching adds a new bucket and is preserved, walking the corpus toward the rare true branch. naive lacks this feedback entirely; value_profile_cmplog adds a substitution stage that here is counterproductive (it loses to plain value_profile), so the enabling technique is the CMP_MAP gradient.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs have value_profile as winner; pair 1 delta is value_profile and pair 2 delta is I2S, and value_profile beats the I2S-equipped variant.
value_profile wins over value_profile_cmplog on the I2S axis, so adding I2S is counterproductive at this branch.
The winner-resolving seed is a SELECT with diverse comparison and arithmetic operators.
Loser-blocking seeds are degenerate digit/operator runs concentrated on '2' and '&'.
The byte diff shows W carries distinct operator bytes per offset while L bytes are concentrated on digits, '2', '&', and '+'.
At the blocker line the winner takes the rare true side once while the loser never does.
value_profile's feedback rewards an input whose CMP-operand pair is closer to matching by adding a new CMP_MAP bucket.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no CMP interception, only edge-counter feedback, so it cannot follow an operand-distance gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile uses only havoc/token mutators with no I2SRandReplace, distinguishing its winning mechanism from input-to-state substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same integer-CMP gate showing value_profile_cmplog reaching the true branch at least as fast as plain value_profile, which would imply I2S substitution (not the CMP_MAP gradient) is the enabling technique.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner-resolving seed is shown, so the claim that operator diversity (rather than incidental query length or a specific literal) is what drives the CMP operand pair toward equality is inferred rather than directly measured.
